title: Invocation Ledger Entry v1
description: Validates a single entry in the high-frequency append-only invocation ledger at `.claude/brain/invocation-ledger.jsonl`. The ledger captures TWO row kinds (per E-B2-6 Q3 oneOf discrimination): (a) `SkillEntry` rows written by `record-skill-invocation.sh` (the existing PostToolUse hook; spec §17.x v1 contract — schema_version + ts + type + name + session_id + invocation_id, no transcript / no arguments / no tool responses); and (b) `DispositionEntry` rows written by `neurogrim disposition record` (NEW at v1; closed-set 4-entry vocabulary per Q1, no free-text justification per Q5, operator-identity discipline per Q6 mirroring §17.6). The ledger remains gitignored runtime state; v1 is per-Brain LOCAL only with no A2A propagation per Q7. Per-entry forward-compat is via the optional `extensions` block (E4-7 pattern, mirrors `trust-budget-v1.schema.json:104-108`); top-level schema validates ONE row at a time. Closed-set discipline on the `Disposition` enum is the v1 vocabulary lock — new vocabulary terms require a spec change with an explicit METHODOLOGY-EVOLUTION entry, same additive promotion path as `culture-manifest-v1.values`, `hat-contract-v1.ToolName`, and `trust-budget-v1.Ecosystem`.

Disposition.description: Closed-vocabulary disposition enum (Q1). Operator's judgment of an agent skill invocation: `accepted` = operator took the suggestion as-is; `rejected` = operator did not take it; `modified` = operator took it with changes; `superseded` = operator chose a different path that addressed the same underlying need. Closed-set v1 (Q1 lock); new vocabulary terms require a spec change with an explicit METHODOLOGY-EVOLUTION entry — same additive discipline as `hat-contract-v1.ToolName` and `culture-manifest-v1.values`. Notably absent: `ignored` (default for everything not dispositioned) and `deferred` (encoding indecision motivates synthetic-disposition spam).

SkillEntry.description: A skill-invocation row written by the PostToolUse hook (`record-skill-invocation.sh:56-58`). Captures name + timestamp + correlation identifiers ONLY; the privacy contract at `docs/invocation-ledger.md:26-39` is normative. The `disposition` field is OPTIONAL forward-compat per Q3 — v1 readers tolerate either presence or absence; the canonical disposition path is a separate `DispositionEntry` row keyed by `invocation_id`.

schema_version.description: Invocation-ledger schema version. Forward-compat per E4-7: any v2 schema (additive per charter discipline #2) requires bumping this const so v1 consumers detect the version mismatch deterministically.

ts.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp generated by the hook at write time.

type.description: Row-kind discriminator for `SkillEntry`. The const value is fixed at v1; future row kinds use other discriminators (e.g., `entry_kind: "disposition"` for `DispositionEntry`).

name.description: Skill name as captured by the PostToolUse hook (e.g., `plan-critic`, `rubber-duck`). The hook extracts this from the envelope's `skill` field.

session_id.description: Claude Code session identifier (UUID-shaped). Used for correlation only; never used for cross-session identity inference.

invocation_id.description: Per-invocation identifier (the hook captures `tool_use_id` from the PostToolUse envelope). MUST be unique within a session. `DispositionEntry` rows reference this value to attach a disposition to a specific invocation.

disposition.description: Optional forward-compat per Q3: a writer that captures the disposition at invocation time MAY include it directly on the skill record. v1 readers tolerate either presence or absence. The canonical disposition path is a separate `DispositionEntry` row — this inline form is reserved for future writers that integrate disposition capture with invocation capture (deferred to v2 per BACKLOG B-23).

extensions.description: E4-7 forward-compat surface. Operators MAY add experimental fields under this object without a schema bump — explicit experimentation space, mirroring `trust-budget-v1.schema.json` and `culture-manifest-v1.schema.json`. Fields under `extensions` are NOT normative; the sensor MUST ignore them at v1; v2 may promote individual extensions to top-level fields with the standard additive-promotion discipline.

DispositionEntry.description: A disposition row written by `neurogrim disposition record` (NEW at v1, E-B2-6 C3). Records the operator's judgment of a prior skill invocation by referencing its `invocation_id`. The 4-entry closed-set `Disposition` vocabulary is normative (Q1). Free-text justification is FORBIDDEN at v1 (Q5 lock — the `additionalProperties: false` discipline below is the structural enforcement; relaxing it requires an explicit charter-level BR-5 conversation per the privacy contract at `docs/invocation-ledger.md:26-39`). Operator identity is REQUIRED at write time per Q6, mirroring §17.6's NEUROGRIM_OPERATOR convention. Disposition rows are LOCAL per Q7 — implementations MUST NOT auto-share disposition entries via A2A in conformant v1 deployments.

schema_version.description: Invocation-ledger schema version. Same forward-compat semantics as `SkillEntry.schema_version`.

ts.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at disposition record time. Monotonically increasing within a single ledger; uniqueness is the writer's responsibility.

entry_kind.description: Row-kind discriminator for `DispositionEntry`. The const value distinguishes this row from a `SkillEntry` (which uses `type: "skill"` instead).

invocation_id.description: REFERENCES the `invocation_id` of an existing `SkillEntry` row in the same ledger (referential integrity is the writer's responsibility; the schema validates one row at a time and cannot enforce cross-row references). The CLI rejects references to operator-calibration finding kinds at parse time per Q6 recursion-guard discipline.

disposition_kind.description: Operator's judgment class. Closed-set 4-entry vocabulary per Q1.

human_operator.description: REQUIRED. Operator handle from `--operator` CLI flag or `NEUROGRIM_OPERATOR` env var (mirrors §17.6 + `domain-calibration-ledger-v1.TriagedEntry.human_operator`). Self-asserted in v1; future versions MAY use cryptographic identity. The writer rejects empty values.

extensions.description: E4-7 forward-compat surface. Same semantics as `SkillEntry.extensions`. Notably this surface MUST NOT be used to smuggle in free-text justification (Q5 lock); doing so violates the privacy contract at `docs/invocation-ledger.md:26-39`. v2 candidates for promotion include `last_disposition_age_days`-style metadata fields.
